findings.evidence: `FullyExchangeable.measurePreserving_shift` at lines 153-164 has `have hcontr : Contractable μ X := ...` on line 156, and the final prefix equality is `exact hcontr n (fun i : Fin n => i.val + 1) ...`; the roadmap also names the natural contractable-level bridge as `measure_map_shift_eq_of_contractable` / `pathSpace_shift_preserving_of_contractable`.
findings.fix: Prove the general theorem first, e.g. `Contractable.measurePreserving_shift` or `measure_map_shift_eq_of_contractable` with `[IsFiniteMeasure μ]` and `hX_meas`, then derive the fully-exchangeable corollary via `contractable_of_exchangeable (hX.exchangeable hX_meas) hX_meas`.
findings.issue: `FullyExchangeable.measurePreserving_shift` assumes full exchangeability even though shift preservation of the path law follows from `Contractable μ X`; the proof immediately builds `hcontr : Contractable μ X` and then uses only `hcontr` for the finite-prefix identities.

summary: One public bridge is stated at an unnecessarily special symmetry level: shift preservation only needs contractability, and the proof already reduces to that stronger statement.
